technique: Password spray - one origin (IP/ASN) against many distinct identities, low attempts per user

trigger.from_breadth: A source IP / ASN with auth FAILURES against >=5 distinct UPNs in the window (broad fan-out, low attempts-per-user = spray signature, distinct from single-user brute force).

telemetry_required: callerIpAddress OR source_ip ; source_ip_asn / Origin_ip enrichment
telemetry_gap_behavior: If UPN is null on the failures, sweep the raw message for result codes + distinct account strings to count the fan-out; if source_ip is null, pivot the count onto ASN or clientAppUsed. Stream total=0 -> REVIEW stream-empty. NEVER clean off a null named field - this is the gap that missed the .in spray on 2026-06-15.

method.goal: Characterize the fan-out from this origin
method.query: (azure_result_signature:FAILURE OR resultSignature:FAILURE) AND (callerIpAddress:"<IP>" OR source_ip:"<IP>") AND NOT azure_result_type:(53003 70043 70044 50173 50140)
method.compute: distinct UPN count; attempts-per-UPN distribution; top azure_result_type (50126 wrong-pw spread across many users = classic spray; 50053 = causing lockouts); distinct clientAppUsed
method.decision_input: LOW attempts (1-3) across MANY distinct users = spray; HIGH attempts against ONE user = brute (hand to a brute module instead)

method.check: clientAppUsed in {Authenticated SMTP, BAV2ROPC, IMAP4, POP3, Exchange ActiveSync, Other clients}
method.decision_input: legacy clientApp = MFA-bypass-capable spray. On casepoint.in this is the active sagar-class campaign; on casepoint.com legacy SMTP is legit for ~8 service accounts -> do not auto-fire .com on legacy alone

method.goal: THE PIVOT - any SUCCESS from this origin for any sprayed UPN
method.compute: success present? which UPN(s); capture appDisplayName, authenticationRequirement, token type
method.decision_input: ANY success from a spraying origin = breach candidate -> chain to credential-stuffing-pivot and impossible-travel for that UPN

method.compute: ASN / org / country of the IP (Origin_ip enrichment). Hosting/VPS/Tor/anonymizer ASN, or origin country outside the GEO-ACL, raises severity.
method.decision_input: spray from a hosting/VPS/Tor ASN or a non-ACL country = stronger; many-countries fan-out (one campaign, many egress IPs) = distributed spray

decision.CRITICAL_conf5: spray (>=5 distinct UPNs) WITH any success, especially legacy clientApp on .in or success token singleFactor
decision.HIGH_conf4: >=10 distinct UPNs sprayed from one origin, no success, but legacy clientApp or non-ACL/hosting origin
decision.MEDIUM_conf3to4: 5-9 distinct UPNs, all failures, ordinary origin = attempted spray (still reported)
decision.FP_kill: one IP fronting many users is a known NAT/Cato egress with NO legacy app and NO success (legit shared egress); smart-lockout-only (50053) with no success = control working

false_positive_checks: Cato egress 140.82.202.196 / 199.27.40.187 / 123.253.153.138 and corporate NAT -> many users behind one IP is expected; require failure codes + (legacy app OR off-hours OR success) before firing
smart-lockout 50053 only, no success = stale-cred lockout, control working -> MEDIUM attempted
alertsind-style single-IP service-account failure (stale svc cred) = separate, not a spray (one user, not many)
